2605.10888.title: Shields to Guarantee Probabilistic Safety in MDPs
2605.10888.paper_summary: Shielding is a prominent model-based technique to ensure safety of autonomous agents. Classical shielding aims to ensure that nothing bad ever happens and comes with strong guarantees about safety and maximal permissiveness. However, shielding systems for probabilistic safety, where something bad is allowed to happen with an acceptable probability, has proven to be more intricate. This paper presents a formal framework that conservatively extends classical shields to probabilistic safety. In this framework, we (i) demonstrate the impossibility of preserving the strong guarantees on safety and permissiveness, (ii) provide natural shields with weaker guarantees, and (iii) introduce offline and online shield constructions ensuring strong safety guarantees. The empirical evaluation highlights the practical advantages of the new shields, as well as their computational feasibility.
2605.10888.paper_summary_zh: 屏蔽是一種突出的基於模型的技術，用以確保自主代理的安全性。傳統的屏蔽旨在確保不會發生任何不好的事情，並對安全性和最大寬容性提供強有力的保證。然而，針對概率安全的屏蔽系統，其中某些不好的事情在可接受的概率下被允許發生，已被證明更為複雜。本文提出了一個正式框架，保守地將傳統屏蔽擴展到概率安全。在這個框架中，我們 (i) 展示了保持安全性和寬容性強保證的不可能性，(ii) 提供了具有較弱保證的自然屏蔽，並 (iii) 介紹了離線和在線屏蔽構造，以確保強安全保證。實證評估突顯了新屏蔽的實際優勢，以及它們的計算可行性。
2605.10888.author: Linus Heck et.al.
2605.10888.authors: Linus Heck, Filip Macák, Roman Andriushchenko, Milan Češka, Sebastian Junges
